page.content: The table presents data from a variable sample size of 129 participants, categorized into responses of "Yes," "No," and "Not sure." The first row, "Would you use a mask even though it is uncomfortable?" shows 93 respondents (72.1%) answered yes, while 8 (6.2%) answered no, and 28 (21.7%) were unsure. The second row, "Would you be screened for TB if you had suggestive symptoms?" indicates that 123 participants (95.4%) would agree, 1 (0.8%) would not, and 5 (3.9%) were uncertain.

cells.cell_text: Would you use a mask even though it is uncomfortable?
Would you be screened for TB if you had suggestive symptoms?
If diagnosed with TB, would you be willing to test for HIV?
If diagnosed with TB, would you be willing to complete treatment?
If you had a condition that predisposed you to contracting TB, would you be willing to change your work environment?
If malnourished, would you be willing to adjust your eating habits?
Would you be willing to provide adequate ventilation and sunlight, regardless of weather conditions?
Would you be willing to teach patients and co-workers about TB prevention?
Would you be willing to attend seminars for TB prevention?

page.content: In the third row, "If diagnosed with TB, would you be willing to test for HIV?" 113 (87.6%) responded yes, 7 (5.4%) no, and 9 (7.0%) were unsure. The fourth entry, "If diagnosed with TB, would you be willing to complete treatment?" reveals 126 (97.7%) yes responses, with no participants (0.0%) answering no, and 3 (2.3%) unsure. The fifth row, "If you had a condition that predisposed you to contracting TB, would you be willing to change your work environment?" shows 104 (80.6%) agreed, 12 (9.3%) disagreed, and 13 (10.1%) were uncertain. For the sixth question, "If malnourished, would you be willing to adjust your eating habits?" 126 (97.7%) said yes, with 2 (1.6%) saying no and 1 (0.8%) unsure. The seventh row, "Would you be willing to provide adequate ventilation and sunlight, regardless of weather conditions?" shows 116 (89.9%) yes, 1 (0.8%) no, and 12 (9.3%) unsure. The eighth question, "Would you be willing to teach patients and co-workers about TB prevention?" has 124 (96.1%) yes, 0 (0.0%) no, and 5 (3.9%) unsure. The final row, "Would you be willing to attend seminars for TB prevention?" has 122 (94.6%) yes, 2 (1.6%) no, and 5 (3.9%) unsure.
